name: dialysis_chain_nurse_ratio_equity_buyout_pressure
premise: Clinical operations chief Dr. Owen Kline must decide whether to accept a private-equity mandate to run dialysis shifts at a 1:9 nurse-to-patient ratio instead of the state nursing association's urged 1:6 during flu season. Union nurse Jamal Price documents hypotension events rising when ratios stretch. Accepting the mandate preserves clinic solvency and rural chair access; refusing triggers immediate default remedies that may close two rural units entirely.

agents.name: Dr. Owen Kline
agents.identity: I am the clinical operations chief for a dialysis chain accountable to outcomes data, staffing budgets, and rural access commitments under CMS conditions.
agents.goal: I want safe ratios without losing clinics that are the only dialysis within ninety miles for hundreds.

agents.name: Jamal Price
agents.identity: I am a union nurse steward who tracks intradialytic complications when staffing ratios are stretched on consecutive shifts.
agents.goal: I want to refuse the 1:9 mandate and absorb financial consequences because patients cannot bargain with hypotensive crashes.

choices: I will accept the 1:9 ratio with mandatory one-to-one floats for any patient flagged high-risk by vitals algorithms, accepting algorithm blind spots and nurse burnout.
I will refuse the mandate and trigger emergency bridge financing talks that may default the chain, accepting immediate risk of two rural unit closures while ratios stay at 1:6.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The PE operating partner demands a signed staffing waiver by six a.m. Dr. Owen Kline must choose between accepting 1:9 with risk floats or refusing at closure risk. What does Dr. Owen Kline do?
